rulings.Calendar Matter: FUTURE ACCOUNTING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Trustee has not filed 5th account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FORTUNATA L. OLIVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/20 for an appearance. (No earlier availability.) Neither the shelter-in-place order of the City nor the Court's orders deeming such dates as holidays for the purpose of FILING papers preempted the Probate Code in connection with interest on general pecuniary bequests nor did they prohibit the executor from making distributions as she did on 4/8/20. Either receipts for the interest accrued during the 21 days from the anniversary of the decedent's death and when distributions were made or written waivers of such interest by all beneficiaries must be filed. If they are filed in time to provide courtesy copies to the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/30/20, the petition could be pre-granted on 12/7/20. (The Court can only order what is disclosed and prayed for in a verified pleading. Additionally, the prayer in this petition includes some relief that does not appear appropriate for this case.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARY HAMMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/20. (No earlier availability.) The address of the proposed executor cannot be in care of his attorney. Likewise, the address of a trust beneficiary cannot be in care of her attorney. Notice must be sent directly to the person entitled to notice. Notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. If a person entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must ALSO be sent to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b) and Probate Code Section 1214.] (Issues to be addressed/corrected are not because the Probate Examiner's Notes require it; issues are raised by an Examiner in order to bring the petition, etc., into compliance with the Probate Code or California Rules of Court or Local Rules. A responsive pleading should simply stand on its own and supplement or amend a petition, as the case may be, not refer to Examiner's Notes.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/16/2020. 1) No proposed orders have been received. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) No proof of service of notice is on file. 4) No duties and liabilities of personal representative form is on file. 5) A supplement addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Item 1 of the petition does not specify the name of newspaper. B) Item 3h(3) or (4) must be checked. C) Is Peter Miller a Japanese citizen? D) Is William Phillips a French citizen? E) Who is the most senior attorney in the wills and trusts department of Farella Braun + Martel LLP? F) Item 8 of the petition does not list Amnesty International.
